title: Ocugen Q1 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Ocugen Q1 2022 Earnings Call Summary. Key Business Developments: Covaxin Clinical Hold: The FDA placed Ocugen's Covaxin Phase 2/3 study on clinical hold following WHO findings regarding manufacturing facility deficiencies at partner Bharat Biotech. However, the company emphasized that the WHO affirmed that the available data indicate Covaxin is effective with no safety concerns identified. Mexico Expansion: Ocugen expanded its commercial territory to include Mexico, now covering all of North America. Covaxin already holds Emergency Use Authorization in Mexico for adults, with a pediatric application (ages 2-18) under review. Ophthalmology Pipeline Progress: The company dosed its second patient in the Phase 1/2 trial for OCU400, a modifier gene therapy targeting retinitis pigmentosa. Management indicated plans to advance OCU410 and OCU200 to clinical trials in 2023. Financial Highlights: Q1 2022 R&D Expenses: $7.9 million (vs. $2.9 million in Q1 2021). Q1 2022 G&A Expenses: $10.1 million (vs. $4.2 million in Q1 2021). Net Loss: Approximately $18 million ($0.09 per share). Cash Position: $129.9 million as of March 31, 2022.
